prompt: Create a Context person page for Patrice Lumumba — the Congolese independence leader assassinated in 1961. Cover his emergence as a political organiser, his vision for a unified independent Congo, his 67-day prime ministership, the Cold War forces that conspired against him (CIA, Belgian intelligence), and why he became a symbol of anti-colonialism across Africa. Make the legacy section honest about what his assassination prevented us from knowing he might have achieved.

assertions.name: File is complete valid HTML with DOCTYPE and closing tags
Uses --purple as primary accent (person page colour)
CSS includes all required design system variables
CIA and Belgian intelligence roles in assassination are addressed with specificity
Legacy section engages honestly with the uncertainty of what might have been
Quick facts box present with biographical details
Connected topics section with typed cards
